title: oenothera cespitosa subsp. brandegeei
intro: Oenothera cespitosa subsp. brandegeei, also known as Brandegee's evening primrose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the evening primrose family and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high levels of beta-carotene and other carotenoids. Consuming the plant may provide health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal preparation.
